formula: Subject + is/are + Adjective-er + than + Object  |  Subject + is/are + more + Adjective + than + Object
when_to_use: Use comparatives to compare exactly TWO things. Short adjectives (1 syllable, most 2-syllable) add -er. Longer adjectives (2+ syllables, especially ending in -ful, -ing, -ous) use 'more' before the adjective instead.
when_to_use_ar: استخدم صيغ المقارنة لمقارنة شيئين بالضبط. الصفات القصيرة (مقطع واحد، أغلب المقاطع الاتنين) بتضيف -er. الصفات الأطول بتستخدم more قبل الصفة.
why_arabic_speakers_struggle: Arabic comparative forms (أكبر من، أغلى من) follow a single consistent pattern regardless of word length. English splits into two different strategies based on syllable count, and learners often either add -er to long words ('expensiver') or 'more' to short words ('more big') incorrectly.

examples.en: This car is bigger than that one.
She is taller than her brother.
This phone is more expensive than that one.
My job is more interesting than my old one.

common_errors.wrong: This phone is expensiver than that one.
common_errors.correct: This phone is more expensive than that one.
common_errors.explanation: 'expensive' has 3 syllables — it needs 'more', not the -er ending.

common_errors.wrong: She is more tall than her brother.
common_errors.correct: She is taller than her brother.
common_errors.explanation: 'tall' is a short, 1-syllable adjective — it takes -er, not 'more'.

common_errors.wrong: This is more good than that.
common_errors.explanation: 'good' is irregular — its comparative is 'better', not 'more good'.

practice_fill_blank.hint: 2 syllables ending in y → change y to i, add -er.

practice_fill_blank.sentence: That movie was ___ (interesting) than the last one.
This restaurant is ___ (good) than that one.
The new phone is ___ (small) than the old one.

quick_rule: 1 syllable or 2-syllable ending in -y → add -er (and change y to i if needed). 2+ syllables (especially -ful/-ing/-ous endings) → use 'more' + adjective. Irregular: good→better, bad→worse, far→farther/further.
quick_rule_ar: مقطع واحد أو مقطعين بينتهوا بـ y → أضف -er. مقطعين فأكتر → استخدم more + الصفة. الشواذ: good→better, bad→worse.
mnemonic: SHORT words get a SHORT ending (-er). LONG words need MORE help (the word 'more').
connect_to_speaking: Practice with your buddy: compare two cities, two foods, or two jobs you know well, using at least 3 different comparative sentences. Mix short and long adjectives on purpose to practice both patterns.
